---
title: "About"
image: gallery/Omer_profile_photo.png
card-style: "summary"
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/omer-tzuk/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/omertzuk
---

I am a physicist and complex-systems researcher currently expanding my work into consciousness science. I completed my B.Sc. in Astronomy & Astrophysics at the University of Bologna, followed by an M.Sc. in Gravitation and Astroparticle Physics (GRAPPA) at the University of Amsterdam.

I then moved into the physics of complex systems at Ben-Gurion University, where my Ph.D. focused on pattern formation and eco-hydrological dynamics in semi-arid ecosystems — studying how dryland vegetation self-organizes as a buffer against aridity and climate stress. Afterward, I worked on spreading phenomena on complex networks during a postdoctoral position at Tel Aviv University.

In between, I spent several years in industry as a data scientist, building knowledge graph systems and LLM-based agents across domains ranging from precision agriculture to psychophysiological health.

My current research moves between environmental complex systems and contemplative neuroscience. I am a postdoctoral fellow at the Contemplative Neuroscience and Neurophenomenology Lab at the University of Haifa, where I study EEG time-series, predictive processing, and altered states of consciousness. In parallel, I hold a postdoctoral position in Yair Mau's Eco-Hydrology Lab at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

Outside the lab, I co-founded [Spirit Tech Hub Israel](https://spirit-tech-hub-il.netlify.app/), a community connecting researchers, developers, and entrepreneurs working at the intersection of technology, consciousness, and human flourishing.

Across these fields, my interest remains constant: understanding how complex systems — ecological, cognitive, and social — self-organize, adapt, and transform.

<!-- Check out my [now page](now.qmd) for semi-frequent updates on what I'm working on and thinking about. -->


<!-- 🤹🏻This blog is a seed nursery for holding ideas, thoughts and projects I'm juggling while I build my [digital garden](https://maggieappleton.com/garden-history). Expect anything from machine learning, data wrangling, reproducible workflows, teaching pedagogy to half-formed opinions on social issues, personal data-viz, and winding journeys through my special interests of the moment. -->

<!-- ## Interviews and Profiles

About me, published by:

-   Australian Research Data Commons (May 2023): [Shaping Research Software: An Interview With Cynthia Huang](https://ardc.edu.au/article/shaping-research-software-an-interview-with-cynthia-huang/)
-   Monash Data Future Institute (Mar 2022): [Monash Data Futures Institute PhD Students](https://www.monash.edu/data-futures-institute/about-us/people/monash-data-futures-institute-phd-students) -->
